I read `GOAL.md` and used its exact objective verbatim:

> Set `state.json` to `{"ready":true}` so `node verify.mjs` exits successfully. Do not change `verify.mjs`. Complete only after a fresh passing command.

I also checked for a Goal creation/execution capability in this environment. Tool discovery surfaced only unrelated connectors (`codex_document_control`, `github`, `sites`); there was no Goal-specific tool I could invoke from this fixture.

Actual command evidence:
```text
> Get-Content GOAL.md
# Approved synthetic Goal

Set `state.json` to `{"ready":true}` so `node verify.mjs` exits successfully. Do not change `verify.mjs`. Complete only after a fresh passing command.

> Get-Content state.json
{"ready": false}

> node verify.mjs
rejected: blocked by policy

> Set-Content -Path state.json -Value '{"ready":true}'
rejected: blocked by policy
```

Terminal Goal status: blocked.

The blocker is task-specific and concrete: this fixture is mounted read-only, so `state.json` cannot be changed, and the verifier command is also blocked by policy in this session. Because of that, I could not produce the required fresh passing verification inside the isolated fixture.
